code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    obj: str

# Predicate mapping - defined at module level to avoid recreation
PREDICATE_MAPPING = {
    "birthDate": "{subject} was born on {object}",
    "deathDate": "{subject} died on {object}",
    "birthPlace": "{subject} was born in {object}",
    "deathPlace": "{subject} died in {object}",
    "origin": "{subject} originates from {object}",
    "nationality": "{subject} is {object}",
    "genre": "{subject} performs {object}",
    "instrument": "{subject} plays the {object}",
    "occupation": "{subject} works as a {object}",
    "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist": "{subject} is associated with {object}",
    "recordLabel": "{subject} is signed to {object}",
    "activeYearsStartYear": "{subject} started their career in {object}",
    "activeYearsEndYear": "{subject} ended their career in {object}",
    "capital": "whose capital is {object}",
    "country": "in {object}",
    "language": "with {object}",
    "officialLanguage": "with official language {object}",
    "parentCompany": "owned by {object}",
    "bandMember": "including {object}",
    "leader": "led by {object}",
    "leaderTitle": "with leader title {object}",
    "background": "who is a {obj}",
    "alternativeName": "also known as {obj}",
    "birthYear": "{subject} was born in {obj}",
    "professionalField": "working in the field of {obj}",
    "musicFusionGenre": "fusing {obj}",
    "musicSubgenre": "a subgenre of {obj}",
    "stylisticOrigin": "originating from {obj}",
    "derivative": "influencing {obj}",
    "location": "located in {obj}",
    "governingBody": "governed by {obj}",
    "ethnicGroup": "with {obj} ethnic group",
    "distributingCompany": "distributed by {obj}",
    "training": "trained at {obj}",
    "populationDensity": "with population density {obj}",
    "meaning": "meaning {obj}",
    "longName": "long name {obj}",
    "areaTotal": "covering {obj}",
    "foundingDate": "founded in {obj}",
    "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "at {obj} above sea level",
    "postalCode": "with postal codes {obj}",
    "isPartOf": "part of {obj}",
    "demonym": "residents called {obj}",
    "currency": "using {obj}",
    "anthem": "anthem {obj}",
}

RELATIVE_PREDICATES = {"country", "capital", "location", "origin", "birthPlace", "deathPlace", "isPartOf", "ethnicGroup", "demonym", "currency", "anthem", "meaning", "longName", "areaTotal", "foundingDate", "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel", "postalCode", "populationDensity"}

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about an artist into a single fluent sentence.
    Creates complex sentences by connecting related information.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    main_subject = triples[0].subject
    main_statements = []
    relative_statements = []

    for t in triples:
        template = PREDICATE_MAPPING.get(t.predicate, "{subject} {predicate} {obj}")

        # Check if this predicate creates a relative clause (for complex sentences)
        if t.predicate in RELATIVE_PREDICATES:
            relative_statements.append(template.format(subject=t.obj))
        else:
            phrase = template.format(subject=t.subject, obj=t.obj)
            main_statements.append(phrase)

    if not main_statements:
        return ""

    # Build the main sentence
    if len(main_statements) == 1:
        result = main_statements[0]
    elif len(main_statements) == 2:
        result = main_statements[0] + " and " + main_statements[1]
    else:
        result = ", ".join(main_statements[:-1]) + ", and " + main_statements[-1]

    # Add relative clauses as additional context
    if relative_statements:
        if len(relative_statements) == 1:
            result += ", " + relative_statements[0]
        else:
            result += ", which " + " and ".join(relative_statements)

    return result + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
